_comment: GENERATED by scripts/generate-agent-kb.ts from docs/content. Do not hand-edit — run `pnpm run generate:agent-kb`.

docs.title: Local development
docs.description: Run the full Carbon stack locally with the crbn CLI, in isolated per-worktree environments.

docs.title: Webhooks
docs.description: Get an HTTP callback the moment a Carbon record is created, changed, or deleted.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.description: Every Carbon term, defined and linked: the manufacturing and accounting vocabulary the rest of the docs lean on.

docs.title: Overview
docs.description: Carbon is a manufacturing system: ERP for the office, MES for the floor. These are the technical docs.

docs.title: Accounting
docs.description: Post invoices to Xero and keep currency exchange rates current automatically.

docs.title: Assistant
docs.description: Bring the Carbon Assistant into Slack, with nonconformance notifications.

docs.title: CAD
docs.description: Sync CAD data from Onshape into Carbon.

docs.title: Email
docs.description: Send outbound email through Carbon-managed Resend or your own SMTP server.

docs.title: Estimating & quoting
docs.description: Pull quotes and bills of material from Paperless Parts into Carbon.

docs.title: Integrations
docs.description: Connect Carbon to your accounting, CAD, project-management, estimating, and messaging tools.
headings: What's available depends on your deployment

docs.title: Project management
docs.description: Link Carbon quality issues to Linear or Jira so engineering tracks them in their own tool.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.description: How Carbon is built: the apps, packages, database, event system, background jobs, and auth behind a deployment.

docs.title: Backups
docs.description: Snapshot a company's data and files, restore it in place with one-click revert, or stand up a new company from a demo template.
headings: Start a company from a template

docs.title: Licensing
docs.description: Carbon is dual-licensed: AGPLv3 for the open core, a commercial license for Enterprise features and for production or hosted use.
headings: You can't resell Carbon as a service
When you need a commercial license

docs.title: AWS with SST
docs.description: Deploy the Carbon stack to your own AWS account with SST: ECS Fargate, ECR, and a WAF.

docs.title: Docker with Caddy
docs.description: Self-host the full Carbon stack on a single Linux VPS with a Docker Swarm and an automatic-HTTPS Caddy reverse proxy.

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.description: Every variable that configures a Carbon instance, grouped by concern. The source of truth is packages/env.

docs.title: Self-hosting
docs.description: Run the whole Carbon stack on your own infrastructure, a single VPS or your own AWS account, instead of Carbon Cloud.

docs.title: Accounting & the ledger
docs.description: How operations become journal entries: accounts, periods, and the postings behind every flow.
headings: "Accounting period is closed. Reopen it before posting."
"Accounting period is locked. Unlock it before posting operational documents."
"Cannot delete journal …: accounting period is closed"
"Posted journal … is immutable and cannot be deleted; reverse it instead"
"Posted journal … is immutable; only the Posted -> Reversed transition is permitted"
"Journal line … is immutable because journal … is posted"

docs.title: Approvals
docs.description: Route purchase orders, quality documents, and suppliers for sign-off before they take effect, with amount-tiered approval rules.

docs.title: Change types
docs.description: Version, Revision, and New Part — the per-affected-item choice that decides what you can edit and what release creates.
headings: Supplier parts on a purchased line

docs.title: Create a change order
docs.description: The entry points that open a change order, the fields on the create form, and how change orders surface back on a part or tool page.
headings: Where change orders surface on a part

docs.title: Change orders
docs.description: How Carbon drafts an engineering change against real methods, reviews it as a diff, and releases it so downstream planning supersedes the versions it replaces.
headings: The affected item is the unit of work

docs.title: Lifecycle & release
docs.description: The five change-order stages, the impact panel, the diff review, and exactly what release does when a change order reaches Done.
headings: Parallel change orders on the same part

docs.title: Categories & actions
docs.description: The two company-scoped lookups that sit alongside change orders — the category list and the reusable action-task templates.

docs.title: Supersession & cutover
docs.description: How a released Revision or New Part phases over, the cutover fields you tune per item, and how MRP and Get Method honor supersession downstream.

docs.title: Fixed assets
docs.description: Capital equipment on the books: acquired, depreciated, and retired.
headings: "Fixed Asset invoice line … has no asset selected"
"Asset is no longer in Draft status"
Depreciation or receipt posting fails with a period error

docs.title: Inventory & locations
docs.description: Where stock lives, how on-hand is tracked, and how it moves.
headings: "Multiple tracked entities in this storage unit — select a specific row to adjust"
"Cannot edit expiry of a consumed tracked entity"

docs.title: Invoices
docs.description: The billing documents — what you charge customers, and what suppliers charge you.
headings: "Cannot void a purchase invoice with payments applied. Reverse the payment first."
"Cannot delete purchase invoice with status … Only Draft invoices can be deleted."
"Cannot modify a confirmed purchase invoice." / "Cannot modify a locked sales invoice."
"Applications can only be edited while the payment is Draft"
"A receipt can only be applied to sales invoices" / "A disbursement can only be applied to purchase invoices"
"A payment can only be applied to its own customer's invoices"

docs.title: Items
docs.description: The master record for everything you make, buy, or stock: parts, materials, tools, and more.

docs.title: Jobs
docs.description: One run of production, covering a single item, a quantity to make, and its own copy of the method.
headings: Make to order vs. make to stock
"Cannot modify a locked job. Reopen it first."

docs.title: Maintenance
docs.description: Keeping work centers running: reactive and preventive maintenance dispatches.

docs.title: Methods & sourcing
docs.description: How Carbon decides whether each part is made, bought, or pulled from stock, and how a job gets its own copy of the recipe.
headings: "An item cannot be added to itself."

docs.title: Planning (MRP)
docs.description: How demand turns into suggested jobs and purchase orders — and where that ends and scheduling begins.
headings: "Failed to create job method for item …"

docs.title: Purchase orders
docs.description: A commitment to a supplier: what to receive, and what you'll be billed for.
headings: "Cannot modify a confirmed purchase order." / "Cannot delete lines on a confirmed purchase order."
"You are not authorized to finalize this purchase order"
"Receiving can only be closed or reopened on a released purchase order"
"This line has no outstanding quantity to receive"
"Receiving is already closed for this line" / "Receiving is not closed for this line"

docs.title: Quality
docs.description: Issues and corrective-action workflows, plus incoming inspection that gates received goods.
headings: "Cannot modify a closed issue. Reopen it first."
"Split quantity exceeds the linked entity quantity"
"Failed to accept lot" / "Failed to reject lot"

docs.title: Quotes
docs.description: A priced offer to a customer — the front of the sales flow.
headings: "Cannot modify a locked quote. Reopen it first."
"An item cannot be added to itself."

docs.title: Receipts
docs.description: Goods coming in: receiving against a purchase order and posting to inventory.
headings: "Cannot void a receipt created by a purchase invoice. Void the invoice instead."

docs.title: Reordering policy
docs.description: How each part decides when and how much to replenish: the policy and the numbers that drive planning.

docs.title: Routings
docs.description: The ordered operations and work centers a part flows through to get made.

docs.title: Sales orders
docs.description: A confirmed customer commitment: what to ship and invoice, tracked line by line.
headings: "Cannot modify a confirmed sales order." / "Cannot modify a locked sales order. Reopen it first."
"Cannot add lines to a locked sales order. Reopen it first." / "Cannot delete lines on a locked sales order. Reopen it first."

docs.title: Shelf life
docs.description: How Carbon stamps expiration dates on tracked stock, and blocks consuming it once expired.
headings: How a date gets set: the three modes
"Cannot consume expired tracked entity" / "Cannot consume expired tracked entities"
"Cannot edit expiry of a consumed tracked entity"

docs.title: Shipments
docs.description: Goods going out — one posting model serving sales orders, returns, and transfers.

docs.title: Storage rules
docs.description: Predicate guards that validate inventory transactions: block with an error, or warn and let the user continue.

docs.title: Suppliers & customers
docs.description: The parties you buy from and sell to, and the ways their records differ.
headings: "Cannot edit a protected customer type" / "Cannot edit a protected supplier type"

docs.title: Traceability
docs.description: Serial and batch tracking, and the genealogy that links what went into what.

docs.title: Work centers
docs.description: Where operations run — the stations and machines, their rates, and the processes they perform.

docs.title: Inside the build
docs.description: How each robot part is made or bought.

docs.title: Revise a part
docs.description: An engineering change, drafted against the real method, reviewed as a diff, and released so planning phases over on its own.
headings: Start with the parts, not the change
Release, and let supersession carry it

docs.title: Acquire and depreciate
docs.description: Put a machine on the books and write its value down over time.

docs.title: Sell or dispose
docs.description: Two ways an asset leaves the books: sold to a buyer, or scrapped.

docs.title: Run the floor
docs.description: Work hits the floor; the floor reports back.

docs.title: How a job costs out
docs.description: Where a job's cost comes from, and how it piles up in work-in-process.

docs.title: Finish, sell, close
docs.description: WIP becomes finished goods, finished goods become COGS, and the job settles.

docs.title: Start with the order
docs.description: A 90-unit robot order becomes real work.

docs.title: Ship, invoice, get paid
docs.description: Fulfill the order, post the shipment and invoice, then record payment.
headings: Three ways to fulfill a line

docs.title: Plan ahead
docs.description: Orders and forecast become jobs and POs.

docs.title: From quote to order
docs.description: An RFQ becomes a quote; an accepted quote becomes a sales order.

docs.title: Receive, match, bill
docs.description: Goods come in, the supplier bill arrives, and Carbon reconciles the two.
headings: Buy by the box, stock by the each

docs.title: From RFQ to PO
docs.description: Shop one item to many suppliers, then turn the winning quote into an order.

docs.title: Quality, trace & ship
docs.description: Prove it's right, ship it, close the books.
